"DISCHARGE DIAGNOSIS: PRIMARY: FALL WITH PROBABLE CONCUSSION SECONDARY: NORMAL PRESSURE HYDROCEPHALUS MOBITZ TYPE II STATUS-POST PACEMAKER PLACEMENT"
"DISCHARGE CONDITION: MENTAL STATUS: CONFUSED - SOMETIMES. LEVEL OF CONSCIOUSNESS: ALERT AND INTERACTIVE. ACTIVITY STATUS: AMBULATORY - REQUIRES ASSISTANCE OR AID OR CANE)

"DISCHARGE INSTRUCTIONS: DEAR MS. , YOU WERE ADMITTED TO BECAUSE YOU HAD A FALL. IT IS UNCLEAR WHY YOU FELL. WE BELIEVE THAT YOU SUFFERED A MILD CONCUSSION SINCE YOU HAVE NO MEMORY BEFORE OR AFTER THE FALL. YOU HAD HEAD IMAGING WHICH SHOWED THAT YOUR VENTRICLES WERE ENLARGED. THEREFORE, YOU WERE EVALUATED BY NEUROSURGERY TO CHECK YOUR VP SHUNT. THEY FOUND THAT YOUR VP SHUNT WAS WORKING PROPERLY. YOU ALSO HAD IMAGING OF YOUR SHUNT WHICH SHOWED THAT IT IS WORKING WELL AND THAT THE SHUNT SETTINGS ARE APPROPRIATE; THE IMAGING WAS UNCHANGED FROM THE LAST TIME YOU HAD YOUR SHUNT EVALUATED. YOUR PACEMAKER WAS ALSO INTERROGATED BY CARDIOLOGY, AND THEY FOUND THAT PACEMAKER WAS WORKING WELL. YOU WERE ALSO EVALUATED BY PHYSICAL THERAPY WHO FELT THAT YOU WOULD BENEFIT FROM DISCHARGE TO A REHABILITATION FACILITY TO HELP YOU WITH YOUR WALKING AND BUILD YOUR STRENGTH. PLEASE FOLLOW-UP WITH YOUR OUTPATIENT PROVIDERS AS INSTRUCTED BELOW. THANK YOU FOR ALLOWING US TO PARTICIPATE IN YOUR CARE. ALL BEST WISHES FOR YOUR RECOVERY."
"CABG X 3, L RADIAL-PDA), PFO CLOSURE, AND REPAIR ANTERIOR LEAFLET TRICUSPID VALVE"

"URINARY RETENTION - HAS HX OF URINARY FREQUENCY AND LEAKAGE FOLLOWED BY UROLOGY. PVR ON ARRIVAL TO FLOOR >300CC. PREV ON ALFUVOZIN STOPPED LAST . CONTINUED VESICARE."
"PANCREATIC CANCER: STAGE 2 DISEASE WITH A 4CM ADENOCARCINOMA IN THE HEAD OF THE PANCREAS AND A POSSIBLE 1.3CM FOCUS IN THE LIVER (NOT BIOPSY-PROVEN). POST RADIATION AND 5 CYCLES GEMCITABINE."

"AMBULATORY STATUS: AMBULATING WITH CANE, AT BASELINE"

"DISCHARGE INSTRUCTIONS: -YOU CAN EXPECT TO SEE OCCASIONAL BLOOD IN YOUR URINE AND TO POSSIBLY EXPERIENCE SOME URGENCY AND FREQUENCY OVER THE NEXT MONTH; THIS MAY BE RELATED TO THE PASSAGE OF STONE FRAGMENTS OR THE INDWELLING URETERAL STENT (IF THERE IS ONE). -THE KIDNEY STONE MAY OR MAY NOT HAVE BEEN REMOVED AND/OR THERE MAY FRAGMENTS/OTHERS STILL IN THE PROCESS OF PASSING. -YOU MAY EXPERIENCE SOME PAIN ASSOCIATED WITH SPASM OF YOUR URETER."
"DISCHARGE INSTRUCTIONS: -URETERAL STENTS MUST BE REMOVED OR EXCHANGED AND THEREFORE IT IS IMPERATIVE THAT YOU FOLLOW-UP AS DIRECTED."
"DISCHARGE INSTRUCTIONS: -DO NOT LIFT ANYTHING HEAVIER THAN A PHONE BOOK (10 POUNDS)"

"DISCHARGE INSTRUCTIONS: -IBUPROFEN (THE INGREDIENT OF ADVIL, MOTRIN, ETC.) MAY BE TAKEN EVEN THOUGH YOU MAY ALSO BE TAKING TYLENOL/ACETAMINOPHEN. YOU MAY ALTERNATE THESE MEDICATIONS FOR PAIN CONTROL."
"DISCHARGE INSTRUCTIONS: -IBUPROFEN SHOULD ALWAYS BE TAKEN WITH FOOD. PLEASE DISCONTINUE TAKING AND NOTIFY YOUR DOCTOR SHOULD YOU DEVELOP BLOOD IN YOUR STOOL (DARK, TARRY STOOLS)"

"DISCHARGE INSTRUCTIONS: -YOU MAY BE DISCHARGED HOME WITH A MEDICATION CALLED PYRIDIUM THAT WILL HELP WITH THE "BURNING" PAIN YOU MAY EXPERIENCE WHEN VOIDING. THIS MEDICATION MAY TURN YOUR URINE BRIGHT ORANGE."

"DISCHARGE INSTRUCTIONS: -COLACE HAS BEEN PRESCRIBED TO AVOID POST SURGICAL CONSTIPATION AND CONSTIPATION RELATED TO NARCOTIC PAIN MEDICATION. DISCONTINUE IF LOOSE STOOL OR DIARRHEA DEVELOPS. COLACE IS A STOOL SOFTENER, NOT A LAXATIVE, AND AVAILABLE OVER THE COUNTER."
"DISCHARGE INSTRUCTIONS: -DO NOT EAT CONSTIPATING FOODS FOR WEEKS, DRINK PLENTY OF FLUIDS TO KEEP HYDRATED. -NO VIGOROUS PHYSICAL ACTIVITY OR SPORTS FOR 4 WEEKS AND WHILE FOLEY CATHETER IS IN PLACE."

"DISCHARGE INSTRUCTIONS: -IF YOU ARE DISCHARGED HOME WITH A FOLEY CATHETER: -PLEASE REFER TO THE PROVIDED NURSING INSTRUCTIONS AND HANDOUT ON FOLEY CATHETER CARE, WASTE ELIMINATION AND LEG BAG USAGE."

"DISCHARGE INSTRUCTIONS: -YOUR FOLEY SHOULD BE SECURED TO THE CATHETER SECURE ON YOUR THIGH AT ALL TIMES UNTIL YOUR FOLLOW UP WITH THE SURGEON. -FOLLOW UP IN 1 WEEK FOR WOUND CHECK AND FOLEY REMOVAL."

"DISCHARGE INSTRUCTIONS: -DO NOT HAVE ANYONE ELSE OTHER THAN YOUR SURGEON REMOVE YOUR FOLEY FOR ANY REASON. -WEAR LARGE FOLEY BAG FOR MAJORITY OF TIME, LEG BAG IS ONLY FOR SHORT-TERM WHEN LEAVING HOUSE."

"CT RECOMMENDED TO BETTER ASSESS IF NEEDED CLINICALLY, PREFERABLY WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST IF NO CONTRAINDICATIONS EXIST."
"SIGNIFICANT INCREASE IN SOFT TISSUE DENSITY IN THE LEFT UPPER LOBE WITH INVASION INTO THE MEDIASTINAL FAT AND NUMEROUS NEW OR ENLARGED CENTRAL NODES CONCERNING FOR PROGRESSION OF MALIGNANCY."

"CXR IMPRESSION: LEFT UPPER LOBE OPACIFICATION WITH MILD VOLUME LOSS CONCERNING FOR PNEUMONIC CONSOLIDATION AND POSSIBLY POST-OBSTRUCTIVE PNEUMONITIS ASSOCIATED WITH A NEW CENTRAL MASS, RADIATION STRICTURE, OR MUCUS PLUG."
"MASS ENCASES THE LEFT PULMONARY VASCULATURE AND THE BRONCHI, SOME OF WHICH ARE NARROWED OR OCCLUDED."
"CONSOLIDATION IN THE LEFT UPPER AND LEFT LOWER LOBES MAY BE RESULT OF POST-OBSTRUCTIVE PNEUMONIA IN THE SETTING OF THE INCREASED SOFT TISSUE MASS."

"POST-OBSTRUCTIVE PNEUMONIA: PT PRESENTED WITH ONE WEEK OF WORSENING DRY COUGH. HIS CXR AND CHEST CT WERE CONCERNING FOR LEFT SIDED SOFT-TISSUE MASS ENCASING THE LEFT VASCULATURE AND BRONCHUS, EXTENDING TO THE ANTERIOR CHEST WALL AND PERICARDIUM. THERE WERE ALSO EVIDENCE OF LEFT SIDED VOLUME LOSS AND CONSOLIDATION, SUGGESTING A POST-OBSTRUCTIVE PNEUMONIA."
"SIGNIFICANT LACTIC ACIDOSIS WITH NO EVIDENCE OF POOR PERFUSION OR SEPSIS. THE LEADING CAUSE OF HIS LACTIC ACIDOSIS WAS METFORMIN TOXICITY."
"CAD: PT HAD EXTENSIVE HISTORY OF CAD. HIS CHEST PAIN IS PLEURITIC, LIKELY A RESULT OF COUGH. HIS EKG IS NOT CONCERNING FOR ACS, AND HIS TROPONIN WAS NEGATIVE."

"ATRIAL FIBRILLATION: PT HAS HISTORY OF A-FIB, AND IS CURRENTLY MANAGEMENT WITH BIVENTRICULAR PACEMAKER WITH MEDICATION CONTROL, INCLUDING DILTIAZEM AND DOFETILIDE. ANTICOAGULATION HELD AS ABOVE AWAITING PROCEDURE."
"DM2: PT HAS REASONABLY CONTROLLED DM2, WITH RECENT A1C OF 7.3. WE HELD HIS ORAL REGIMEN AND STARTED SLIDING SCALE INSULIN."
"CODE STATUS: DNR/DNI (CONFIRMED WITH PT AND WIFE)"
"AMOXICILLIN AND CLAVULANATE 1,000/62.5 MG BID FOR 7 DAYS - STARTED ALBUTEROL INHALER AS NEEDED EVERY 6 HOURS FOR COUGH / WHEEZE - STARTED GUAIFENESIN / CODEINE AS NEEDED EVERY 6 HOURS FOR COUGH"

"PT WILL BE CALLED NEXT WEEK FOR SCHEDULE OF THE BRONCHOSCOPY - PT WILL BE CALLED FOR PCP APPOINTMENT NEXT WEEK - PT IS AWARE OF THE RADIOLOGY STUDY FINDINGS MEDICATIONS ON ADMISSION: PLAVIX 75 MG QD FUROSEMIDE 20 MG QD DILTIAZEM CR (CARTIA) 240 MG QD D"

"LEFT UPPER LOBE OPACIFICATION WITH MILD VOLUME LOSS CONCERNING FOR PNEUMONIC CONSOLIDATION AND POSSIBLY POST-OBSTRUCTIVE PNEUMONITIS ASSOCIATED WITH A NEW CENTRAL MASS, RADIATION STRICTURE, OR MUCUS PLUG. MORE CENTRAL DENSER OPACITY MAY REPRESENT MASS OR PARTICULARLY DENSE AREA OF CONSOLIDATION. CT IS RECOMMENDED TO BETTER ASSESS IF NEEDED CLINICALLY, PREFERABLY WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST IF NO CONTRAINDICATIONS EXIST."

"SIGNIFICANT INCREASE IN SOFT TISSUE DENSITY IN THE LEFT UPPER LOBE WITH INVASION INTO THE MEDIASTINAL FAT AND NUMEROUS NEW OR ENLARGED CENTRAL NODES CONCERNING FOR PROGRESSION OF MALIGNANCY. ADDITIONALLY A PARENCHYMAL RIGHT MIDDLE LOBE NODULE IS INCREASED SINCE. THE MASS ENCASES THE LEFT PULMONARY VASCULATURE AND THE BRONCHI, SOME OF WHICH ARE NARROWED OR OCCLUDED. CONSOLIDATION IN THE LEFT UPPER AND LEFT LOWER LOBES MAY BE RESULT OF POST-OBSTRUCTIVE PNEUMONIA IN THE SETTING OF THE INCREASED SOFT TISSUE MASS. THE MASS IS IN CLOSE CONTACT WITH THE PERICARDIUM, AND PERICARDIAL INVOLVEMENT CANNOT BE EXCLUDED. SMALL-MODERATE LEFT PLEURAL EFFUSION."

"POST-OBSTRUCTIVE PNEUMONIA: PT PRESENTED WITH ONE WEEK OF WORSENING DRY COUGH. HIS CXR AND CHEST CT WERE CONCERNING FOR LEFT SIDED SOFT-TISSUE MASS ENCASING THE LEFT VASCULATURE AND BRONCHUS, EXTENDING TO THE ANTERIOR CHEST WALL AND PERICARDIUM. THERE WERE ALSO EVIDENCE OF LEFT SIDED VOLUME LOSS AND CONSOLIDATION, SUGGESTING A POST-OBSTRUCTIVE PNEUMONIA. THESE FINDINGS ARE VERY CONCERNING FOR EITHER A NEW PRIMARY MALIGNANCY OR LESS LIKELY A RECURRENCE FROM THE PREVIOUS SMALL CELL CARCINOMA OF THE LUNG."

"LACTIC ACIDOSIS: PT PRESENTED WITH SIGNIFICANT LACTIC ACIDOSIS WITH NO EVIDENCE OF POOR PERFUSION OR SEPSIS. THE LEADING CAUSE OF HIS LACTIC ACIDOSIS WAS METFORMIN TOXICITY. HIS LACTATE LEVEL NORMALIZED AFTER THE DISCONTINUATION OF METFORMIN AND GENTLE FLUID REPLETION. WE DECREASED HIS METFORMIN DOSE TO 500 MG BID AT DISCHARGE."
